evidence_before: Installer/profile lane review during greenloop found no fail-closed validation for unready hybrid-kimi selections.
failure_ledger.remaining_risk: Kimi remains optional on this machine because no KIMI_API_KEY is configured.
failure_ledger.root_cause: The installers summarized profile truth but never validated the selected profile readiness before proceeding.
failure_ledger.symptom: Selecting the hybrid Kimi install profile could continue into launcher generation even when the remote lane was not configured.

evidence_before: The batch contract lacked requested_profile forwarding and validate_install_profile invocation.
failure_ledger.remaining_risk: Windows parity still depends on the same remote lane being configured when hybrid-kimi is selected.
failure_ledger.root_cause: The batch launcher path did not thread NULLA_INSTALL_PROFILE into the provider-truth helper and did not call the profile validator.
failure_ledger.symptom: The Windows install path could drop the requested install profile when building profile truth and skip explicit readiness validation.

evidence_before: Default provider probe output mixed unsupported remote ideas into the normal recommendation surface.
failure_ledger.remaining_risk: Unsupported remote ideas still exist behind --show-unsupported for explicit operator review.
failure_ledger.root_cause: Unsupported remote concepts were included in the default stack surface instead of being isolated behind an explicit flag.
failure_ledger.symptom: The default provider probe could surface unsupported remote ideas like Tether or QVAC alongside real install lanes.

evidence_before: Earlier greenloop reruns replaced blocked output directories in place.
failure_ledger.remaining_risk: Preserved bundles can still contain local absolute paths unless the operator chooses a sanitized output root.
failure_ledger.root_cause: The acceptance scripts replaced prior output directories in place without preserving failed bundles first.
failure_ledger.symptom: A fresh green run could overwrite the previous blocked or red proof bundle and destroy the first-red evidence.

evidence_before: Repo search showed no tracked greenloop concurrency probe or generator for reports/greenloop/concurrency.csv.
failure_ledger.remaining_risk: This probe measures single-node API concurrency only; it does not profile helper-mesh or WAN traffic.
failure_ledger.root_cause: Greenloop stopped at llm_eval and never added a first-class concurrency probe to the proof path.
failure_ledger.symptom: The checklist required a real concurrency/performance lane, but the repo had no canonical command to generate concurrency.csv.

fix_ledger.safety_notes: The validator only blocks clearly unready remote profiles; default local-only installs remain unchanged.
fix_ledger.summary: Added explicit install-profile validation so shell and Windows launchers fail closed when hybrid-kimi is selected without a ready remote lane.

fix_ledger.safety_notes: Unsupported ideas still remain visible behind --show-unsupported for deliberate operator review.
fix_ledger.summary: Separated unsupported remote ideas from the default provider probe surface so install recommendations stay grounded in real lanes.

fix_ledger.safety_notes: Preservation only triggers when the prior bundle is blocked or non-green, so clean reruns do not explode the artifact tree.
fix_ledger.summary: Preserved previous blocked or red llm_eval and local-acceptance bundles before writing a new run so the first-red evidence survives reruns.

fix_ledger.safety_notes: The probe only hits /api/chat with isolated workspaces and does not mutate shared user state.
fix_ledger.summary: Added a canonical greenloop concurrency probe that records success rate, throughput, and latency percentiles at worker counts 1, 2, and 4.

gates.notes: Fresh dev venv install and entrypoint/import smoke passed.
Fresh runtime+dev venv install and import smoke passed.

gates.command: python -m compileall -q apps core installer ops tests

gates.notes: Only warning was the existing setuptools project.license deprecation.
Live lane stayed green; llm_eval still updates docs/LLM_ACCEPTANCE_REPORT.md during the run, which makes the acceptance build stamp dirty.

metadata.change_summary: Greenloop rerun on 15948c7 closed install/provider proof gaps, added a canonical concurrency lane, and regenerated the acceptance bundle on the current head.

notes: Primary local model detected by provider probe.
Locked acceptance model and helper lane for local proof.
Profile is visible only as a configured follow-up; this machine has no KIMI_API_KEY.

signoff.notes: All required gates in the greenloop checklist are green on commit 15948c7. Remaining risk is mostly proof-tooling and unlanded local slices, not the landed runtime/install/provider surface.
required_followups: Keep Beta2_Website, the public_hive split, and the runtime_task_rail split out of main until they get isolated proof packs.
Decouple llm_eval from docs/LLM_ACCEPTANCE_REPORT.md writes during proof runs so clean-checkout acceptance can stamp non-dirty builds.
If Kimi is meant to be first-class on this machine, configure KIMI_API_KEY and rerun the provider probe and install validator.
